rxn_class_summary: Electrochemical syn-diformyloxylation (protected syn-1,2-diol formation) of vinylarenes using DMF as the oxygen/formyl source and TFA as a co-nucleophile under undivided-cell anodic oxidation.
electrochemical_role: Anodic single-electron oxidation of the vinylarene to a benzylic-stabilized alkene radical cation (Eox ~1.15-1.75 V vs SCE); a second anodic SET oxidizes the β-DMF-adduct radical to a benzylic carbocation. TFA/TFA- couple at the cathode produces H2 and regenerates TFA.

key_intermediates.name: β-(N,N-dimethyl-iminium-oxy) benzylic radical (C) from nucleophilic addition of DMF oxygen to radical cation
benzylic carbocation (D) formed by second anodic SET oxidation of C
trifluoroacetoxy-iminium adduct (E/F, isolable) — major path via TFA- capture of D
stereoinverted carbocation/iminium intermediate (G) leading to syn-product via DMF attack with stereoinversion
anti-product H' (minor path, direct DMF attack on D)

electron_flow: Anode oxidizes the vinylarene to a radical cation; the carbonyl oxygen of DMF adds to the benzylic position giving an α-iminium-oxy carbon radical, which is oxidized again at the anode to a benzylic cation. Trifluoroacetate captures this cation (major path) to give an isolable α-OCOCF3 iminium that undergoes SN1-like ionization and stereoinverted nucleophilic attack by a second DMF, followed by hydrolysis with H2(18)O to deliver the syn-diformate; TFA is regenerated and protons are reduced at the Pt cathode to H2.
rate_determining_step: Initial anodic single-electron oxidation of the vinylarene to its radical cation (alkene Eox 1.15–1.75 V; thermodynamically more accessible than DMF oxidation at 2.1 V).

probe_reagents.name: DMF-d7
probe_reagents.observation: Both formyl groups in the product are >99% deuterated, confirming that both O-CHO units originate from DMF, not from formic acid or water.

probe_reagents.name: formic acid-d2 (DCOOD)
probe_reagents.observation: No deuterium incorporation in product formyl groups, ruling out hydrolytically generated HCOOH as the formyl source.

probe_reagents.name: H2(18)O
probe_reagents.observation: Doubly 18O-labeled diformate (>69%) is obtained; hydrolysis to diol removes the 18O label, showing the formyloxy oxygens (and thus the diol oxygens) originate from DMF and are only delivered via hydrolytic workup of the iminium intermediates.

probe_reagents.name: (2-phenylcyclopropyl)-substituted vinyl (radical clock, 30)
probe_reagents.observation: Ring-opened diformyloxylation product 31 (49%) is obtained, implicating a benzylic radical intermediate.

probe_reagents.name: cis- vs trans-β-methylstyrene (35)
probe_reagents.observation: Both isomers give predominantly syn-diformate but with different dr (trans-35: 10:1; cis-35: 1:3 favoring 19'), showing partial memory of olefin geometry and supporting a stepwise cation/iminium pathway with stereoinversion at the second nucleophilic attack.

probe_reagents.name: cyclic voltammetry of styrene 1 ± TFA ± DMF
probe_reagents.observation: Styrene oxidation feature at Ep/2 ≈ 1.7 V vs SCE; DMF oxidation only at 2.1 V; no anodic shift with TFA/DMF additives, confirming direct anodic oxidation of the alkene rather than mediation through DMF.

notes: Two competing pathways from the benzylic cation D are proposed: a minor 'direct' path where DMF attacks D to give the anti-product H', and a major path where trifluoroacetate first traps D to form isolable α-OCOCF3 iminium F, which then ionizes and is attacked by DMF with stereoinversion to give the syn-product H. The unusual syn-selectivity (and its partial dependence on starting alkene geometry) is rationalized by this TFA-mediated SN1-with-inversion sequence rather than by a concerted cyclic intermediate as in Woodward/Prévost/OsO4 chemistry. With a 1,3-diene (32), mono-oxidation gives a 3-OCOCF3 allylic intermediate (33) which can be converted to the diformate 34 by treatment with DMF/H2O, further supporting F-type intermediates.
